intent:
Caught in the rain, the narrator takes shelter at a shop. The shoes are cold and wet. The shopkeeper is holding a towel; the towel is warm. The narrator takes that towel — the shoes are still wet. Sensory contrast (falling-cold-rain → cold+wet shoes → warm dry towel → still-wet shoes) and a small kindness from a stranger. Anchor is the towel: held by shopkeeper at s3, sensory-described at s4, transferred to narrator's hand at the closer.

PREMISE CONTRACT (story 7)
  one_sentence_event:    The narrator takes a warm towel a shopkeeper is holding because the rain has wet the narrator's shoes.
  change_of_state:       at s0 the narrator stands rained-on with no towel; at the closer the narrator holds the towel (anchor transferred from shopkeeper → narrator). Shoes remain wet — the relief is partial.
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a classroom / station / park_bench story; this is rainy_doorway (first appearance in the corpus).
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is the towel. It has never appeared in any story (last 5 anchors: apple, egg, paper, pencil, umbrella).
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: action+state-contrast (`そのタオルを取りますが、靴は濡れています。`). NOT a single-clause `〜を取りました` past mirror of story 5, NOT a `〜で〜の〜を書きました` past chain of story 4, NOT a `〜だね` confirmation tag of story 6.
  why_not_filler:        narrator (anchor receiver, sensory experiencer); shopkeeper (sole giver — the kindness must come from a person, not the narrator); rain (sets the scene, force behind the wet shoes); shoes (the body-adjacent discomfort the towel partially answers); towel (anchor — transferred from shopkeeper to narrator; warmth contrasts with cold rain & wet shoes). Each beat earns its slot.
  obligations_absorbed:  must-reinforce N5_ga_but absorbed in closer (`取りますが、`); must-reinforce N5_kosoado absorbed in closer (`そのタオル`). Three new grammar points introduced: N5_dakara (N5_kara_because) in s1, N5_dictionary_form (N5_dictionary_form) in s0 via attributive 「降る雨」, and N5_i_adj_te (N5_i_adj_te) in s2 via 「冷たくて、」 — all naturally embedded, not bolted on.

GRAMMAR INTRO NOTE: brief recommends N5_dictionary_form as recommended[0]; introduced via attributive 「降る雨は冷たいです」 in s0 (verb modifies noun directly — the most learner-frontable use of the dictionary form). N5_kara_because (N5_dakara) introduced via 「雨だから、」 in s1. N5_i_adj_te (N5_i_adj_te) introduced via 「冷たくて、」 in s2 (links cold + wet attributes onto same subject — a natural i-adj te-form linking site).

NATURALNESS NOTES: §E.7.5 round-trip 1 caught REDUNDANT-PEDAGOGY in over-marked subject pronouns (`私は店にいます`, `私の靴も冷たい`); fixed by dropping `私` (narrator implicit). Round 2 caught WRONG-VERB on `出す` for the shopkeeper-towel act; fixed by switching to ongoing-state `持っています` (stative; the implicit social act of holding-out is conveyed by the next-sentence transfer). Above-tier verbs `くれる` (N4), `受け取る` (N3), `差し出す` (N1) are unavailable under the N5 cap and 2-override budget already absorbed by `タオル` and `濡れる`. The remaining `取る` for narrator-receives is in-tier and natural (`hand-to-hand take` is exactly what `取る` covers — N5 native usage).

LEXICAL OVERRIDES NOTE: タオル (N3, no nf-band; the anchor; seed-prescribed) and 濡れる (N4, nf41; seed-prescribed; needed for the wet-shoes state). Both are core to the rainy_doorway scene; without them the story cannot be told. Two overrides used; no third.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY (per pipeline/tools/forbid.py 7):
  scene_class:    classroom, park_bench, station   → using `rainy_doorway` (fresh).
  anchor_object:  apple, egg, paper, pencil, umbrella → using `towel` (fresh).
  closer_shape:   noun-NO-noun-DESU-P:ね (story 6), noun-WA-noun-DE-noun-NO-noun-WO-noun (story 4), noun-WA-noun-WO-noun (story 5) → closer is two-clause action+state-contrast (`〜が、〜濡れています`), distinct from all three.
  opening_seqs:   {私/学校/います}, {私/母/駅}, {公園/椅子/母} → opening is `降る/雨/冷たい` (rain-first attributive opening, no human subject).

sentences.en: Because of the rain, I am at a shop.

sentences.jp: 靴は冷たくて、濡れています。
sentences.en: My shoes are cold and wet.

sentences.jp: 店の人がタオルを持っています。
sentences.en: The shopkeeper is holding a towel.

sentences.jp: そのタオルを取りますが、靴は濡れています。
sentences.en: I take that towel, but my shoes are still wet.
